questions.q: In which year was the film Pather Panchali released?
questions.explanation: Pather Panchali, Satyajit Ray's debut feature, was released in 1955.

questions.q: Who directed the 1955 film Pather Panchali?
questions.explanation: Pather Panchali was the directorial debut of Satyajit Ray.

questions.q: In which Indian language was the film Pather Panchali made?
questions.explanation: Pather Panchali is a Bengali-language film.

questions.q: Pather Panchali is the first film of which famous trilogy directed by Satyajit Ray?
questions.explanation: Pather Panchali is the first film in Satyajit Ray's celebrated Apu Trilogy.

questions.q: Who composed the music for the 1955 film Pather Panchali?
questions.explanation: The sitar maestro Ravi Shankar composed the score for Pather Panchali.

questions.q: The film Pather Panchali was adapted from a Bengali novel written by which author?
questions.explanation: Pather Panchali was based on the 1929 novel by Bibhutibhushan Bandopadhyay.

questions.q: What is the name of the young boy who is the central child character in Pather Panchali?
questions.explanation: The story centres on the boy Apu, whose journey continues through the Apu Trilogy.

questions.q: The title of the film Pather Panchali is most commonly translated into English as which phrase?
questions.explanation: Pather Panchali is commonly translated as 'Song of the Little Road'; 'The Unvanquished' and 'The World of Apu' are the English titles of the trilogy's sequels.

questions.q: Which musical instrument is most prominently featured in Ravi Shankar's score for Pather Panchali?
questions.explanation: Ravi Shankar, a sitar virtuoso, gave the film its distinctive sitar-led score.

questions.q: In which Indian state is the rural setting of Pather Panchali located?
questions.explanation: The film is set in a village in Bengal, and its production was partly funded by the West Bengal government.

questions.q: Which government body provided partial funding that helped complete the production of Pather Panchali?
questions.explanation: Production stalled for lack of funds until the West Bengal government stepped in with financing.

questions.q: At the 1956 Cannes Film Festival, what award did Pather Panchali win?
questions.explanation: Pather Panchali won the 'Best Human Document' prize at Cannes in 1956.

questions.q: Who plays Sarbajaya, the mother of the family, in Pather Panchali?
questions.explanation: Karuna Banerjee portrayed Sarbajaya, Apu's mother, in the film.

questions.q: In Pather Panchali, what is the name of Apu's elder sister?
questions.explanation: Apu's elder sister in the film is named Durga.

questions.q: Which elderly relative living with the family is a memorable character in Pather Panchali?
questions.explanation: The aged aunt Indir Thakrun, played by Chunibala Devi, is one of the film's most memorable figures.

questions.q: Who served as the cinematographer on Pather Panchali, marking a celebrated debut behind the camera?
questions.explanation: Subrata Mitra, then a newcomer, photographed Pather Panchali and became one of India's most acclaimed cinematographers.

questions.q: Approximately how long did the making of Pather Panchali take, owing to repeated funding interruptions?
questions.explanation: Filming stretched over roughly three years because shooting kept halting whenever money ran out.

questions.q: What is the profession of Harihar, the father of the family, in Pather Panchali?
questions.explanation: Harihar works as a priest while hoping to make a living as a writer and poet.

questions.q: Pather Panchali was the first Indian film to gain widespread international recognition at major festivals abroad. What broad film movement is it associated with?
questions.explanation: The film is regarded as a foundational work of India's parallel cinema, or Indian New Wave.

questions.q: Before becoming a director, in what profession did Satyajit Ray, maker of Pather Panchali, work?
questions.explanation: Ray worked as a commercial artist and illustrator at an advertising agency before directing Pather Panchali.

questions.q: Which two films complete the Apu Trilogy that begins with Pather Panchali?
options: Aranyer Din Ratri and Ashani Sanket
questions.explanation: The Apu Trilogy comprises Pather Panchali (1955), Aparajito (1956), and Apur Sansar (1959).

questions.q: Which neorealist film, seen by Satyajit Ray in London, is often cited as inspiring his realist approach to Pather Panchali?
questions.explanation: Seeing Vittorio De Sica's Italian neorealist film Bicycle Thieves in London strongly influenced Ray's naturalistic style.

questions.q: Chunibala Devi, who played the old aunt Indir Thakrun in Pather Panchali, was notable for what reason at the time of filming?
options: She was an elderly former stage actress brought out of obscurity
She had never acted before in her life
questions.explanation: Chunibala Devi was a very elderly former theatre actress whom Ray sought out specifically for the role.

questions.q: Which New York museum hosted an early American showing of Pather Panchali in 1955, the same year as the film's release?
questions.explanation: Pather Panchali was shown at the Museum of Modern Art (MoMA) in New York in 1955, around the time of its release, with MoMA closely associated with the film's early international exposure.

questions.q: Which member of Satyajit Ray's regular crew edited Pather Panchali?
questions.explanation: Dulal Dutta edited Pather Panchali and went on to cut nearly all of Ray's later films.

questions.q: A celebrated sequence in Pather Panchali shows Apu and Durga running through tall grass to glimpse what symbol of modernity?
questions.explanation: The famous scene of the children racing to see a passing train is one of the film's most iconic images.

questions.q: Who designed the art direction and sets for Pather Panchali, becoming a long-time Ray collaborator?
questions.explanation: Bansi Chandragupta was the art director on Pather Panchali and many subsequent Ray films.

questions.q: At the National Film Awards of India, what top honour did Pather Panchali receive?
options: President's Gold Medal for the All India Best Feature Film
questions.explanation: Pather Panchali won the President's Gold Medal for the All India Best Feature Film at the National Film Awards.

questions.q: Satyajit Ray reportedly funded the early shooting of Pather Panchali partly by doing what?
options: Pawning his wife's jewellery and his own insurance
questions.explanation: Ray financed initial shooting by selling his insurance policy and pawning his wife's jewellery before government funding arrived.

questions.q: In what form did Bibhutibhushan Bandopadhyay's source story for Pather Panchali first appear before its novel publication?
questions.explanation: The story was first serialized in a Bengali periodical before appearing as a novel in 1929.
